diagnostics.message: index expression not provably within array bounds
notes: array 'Arr' has index range Idx (1 .. 10).

index expression 'Idx (A + B)' has type Integer, with range
[Integer'First .. Integer'Last].

static analysis cannot establish that the index is within
[1 .. 10] on all execution paths.
rule: D27 Rule 2 (Provable Index Safety)

per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.2 paragraph 131:
"The index expression in an indexed_component shall be provably
within the array object's index bounds at compile time."

per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.2 paragraph 132:
"If neither condition holds, the program is nonconforming and
the implementation shall reject it with a diagnostic identifying
the indexed_component and the unresolvable bound relationship."

result.stderr:
neg_rule1_index_fail.safe:19:14: error: index expression not provably within array bounds
  |
  | 19 |       return Arr (Idx (A + B));  -- ERROR: A + B may not be in 1..10
  |    |              ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
  |
  note: array 'Arr' has index range Idx (1 .. 10).
  note: index expression 'Idx (A + B)' has type Integer, with range
        [Integer'First .. Integer'Last].
  note: static analysis cannot establish that the index is within
        [1 .. 10] on all execution paths.
  note: rule: D27 Rule 2 (Provable Index Safety)
  note: per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.2 paragraph 131:
        "The index expression in an indexed_component shall be provably
        within the array object's index bounds at compile time."
  note: per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.2 paragraph 132:
        "If neither condition holds, the program is nonconforming and
        the implementation shall reject it with a diagnostic identifying
        the indexed_component and the unresolvable bound relationship."
  suggestion: add a bounds check before indexing:
        if A + B >= 1 and then A + B <= 10 then
           return Arr (Idx (A + B));
        end if;

diagnostics.message: intermediate overflow in integer expression
notes:
static range analysis determines that the subexpression (A * B)
has range [0 .. 99_999_999_999_999_980_000_000_000_000_001]
which exceeds the 64-bit signed range
[-9_223_372_036_854_775_808 .. 9_223_372_036_854_775_807].
rule: D27 Rule 1 (Wide Intermediate Arithmetic)

per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.1 paragraph 129:
"If a conforming implementation cannot establish, by sound static
range analysis, that every intermediate subexpression of an integer
arithmetic expression stays within the 64-bit signed range, the
expression shall be rejected with a diagnostic."

  |    |              ^~~~~

        has range [0 .. 99_999_999_999_999_980_000_000_000_000_001]
        which exceeds the 64-bit signed range

  note: A has range [0 .. 9_999_999_999_999_999]
  note: B has range [0 .. 9_999_999_999_999_999]
  note: rule: D27 Rule 1 (Wide Intermediate Arithmetic)
  note: per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.1 paragraph 129:
        "If a conforming implementation cannot establish, by sound static
        range analysis, that every intermediate subexpression of an integer
        arithmetic expression stays within the 64-bit signed range, the

diagnostics.message: divisor not provably nonzero
notes:
right operand 'Denom' has type Integer (range -9_223_372_036_854_775_808 .. 9_223_372_036_854_775_807),
which includes zero.

no preceding conditional or subtype constraint establishes
Denom /= 0 on all paths reaching this division.
rule: D27 Rule 3 (Division by Provably Nonzero Divisor)

per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.3 paragraph 133:
"The right operand of the operators /, mod, and rem shall be
provably nonzero at compile time."

per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.3 paragraph 134:
"If none of the conditions in paragraph 133 holds, the program
is nonconforming and a conforming implementation shall reject
the expression with a diagnostic."
suggestions:
or use a positive subtype that excludes zero:
type Positive_Value is range 1 .. 100;

  |
  | 17 |       return Integer (N) / Denom;  -- ERROR: Denom may be zero
  |    |                            ^~~~~
  |
  note: right operand 'Denom' has type Integer (range -9_223_372_036_854_775_808 .. 9_223_372_036_854_775_807),
        which includes zero.
  note: no preceding conditional or subtype constraint establishes
        Denom /= 0 on all paths reaching this division.
  note: rule: D27 Rule 3 (Division by Provably Nonzero Divisor)
  note: per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.3 paragraph 133:
        "The right operand of the operators /, mod, and rem shall be
        provably nonzero at compile time."
  note: per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.3 paragraph 134:
        "If none of the conditions in paragraph 133 holds, the program
        is nonconforming and a conforming implementation shall reject
        the expression with a diagnostic."
  suggestion: add a guard before the division:
        if Denom /= 0 then
           return Integer (N) / Denom;
        else
           return 0;  -- or handle the zero case
        end if;
  suggestion: or use a positive subtype that excludes zero:
        type Positive_Value is range 1 .. 100;

diagnostics.message: dereference of possibly null access value
notes: P is of type Value_Ptr (access Value), which does not exclude null.

does not exclude null."

  note: rule: D27 Rule 4 (Not-Null Dereference)
  note: per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.4 paragraph 136:
        "Dereference of an access value shall require the access subtype
        to be not null. A conforming implementation shall reject any
        dereference where the access subtype at the point of dereference
        does not exclude null."
  suggestion: use a "not null access" subtype, or add an explicit null check:
        if P /= null then
           return P.all;
        end if;

neg_rule1_overflow.safe:19:14: error: intermediate overflow in integer expression
  |
  | 19 |       return A * B;  -- ERROR: intermediate may exceed 64-bit range
  |    |              ^~~~~
  |
  note: static range analysis determines that the subexpression (A * B)
        has range [0 .. 99_999_999_999_999_980_000_000_000_000_001]
        which exceeds the 64-bit signed range
        [-9_223_372_036_854_775_808 .. 9_223_372_036_854_775_807].
  note: A has range [0 .. 9_999_999_999_999_999]
  note: B has range [0 .. 9_999_999_999_999_999]
  note: rule: D27 Rule 1 (Wide Intermediate Arithmetic)
  note: per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.1 paragraph 129:
        "If a conforming implementation cannot establish, by sound static
        range analysis, that every intermediate subexpression of an integer
        arithmetic expression stays within the 64-bit signed range, the
        expression shall be rejected with a diagnostic."

neg_rule2_oob.safe:15:14: error: index expression not provably within array bounds
  |
  | 15 |       return Arr (Slot (Idx));  -- ERROR: Idx may not be in 0..7
  |    |              ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
  |
  note: array 'Arr' has index range Slot (0 .. 7).
  note: index expression 'Slot (Idx)' has type Integer, with range
        [Integer'First .. Integer'Last].
  note: static analysis cannot establish that the index is within
        [0 .. 7] on all execution paths.
  note: rule: D27 Rule 2 (Provable Index Safety)
  note: per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.2 paragraph 131:
        "The index expression in an indexed_component shall be provably
        within the array object's index bounds at compile time."
  note: per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.2 paragraph 132:
        "If neither condition holds, the program is nonconforming and
        the implementation shall reject it with a diagnostic identifying
        the indexed_component and the unresolvable bound relationship."
  suggestion: add a bounds check before indexing:
        if Idx >= 0 and then Idx <= 7 then
           return Arr (Slot (Idx));
        end if;

neg_rule3_zero_div.safe:14:16: error: divisor not provably nonzero
  |
  | 14 |       return A / B;  -- ERROR: B may be zero
  |    |                  ^
  |
  note: right operand 'B' has type Value (range -100 .. 100),
        which includes zero.
  note: no preceding conditional or subtype constraint establishes
        B /= 0 on all paths reaching this division.
  note: rule: D27 Rule 3 (Division by Provably Nonzero Divisor)
  note: per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.3 paragraph 133:
        "The right operand of the operators /, mod, and rem shall be
        provably nonzero at compile time."
  note: per spec/02-restrictions.md section 2.8.3 paragraph 134:
        "If none of the conditions in paragraph 133 holds, the program
        is nonconforming and a conforming implementation shall reject
        the expression with a diagnostic."
  suggestion: add a guard before the division:
        if B /= 0 then
           return A / B;
        else
           return 0;  -- or handle the zero case
        end if;
  suggestion: or use a positive subtype that excludes zero:
        type Positive_Value is range 1 .. 100;
